I came here to clear my mind, take a break from everything, and just breathe for a while. Standing there, looking out over the Seattle skyline with Mount Rainier in the background, reminded me how important it is to pause and appreciate the moment.
The view is absolutely stunning, especially around sunset. It’s peaceful, not crowded, and offers one of the best panoramic views of the city. Whether you’re a visitor or a local, this is the perfect place to reflect, relax, or simply enjoy the beauty of Seattle.
Sometimes all you need is a quiet place with an incredible view—and Kerry Park delivered exactly that.
It's easy to get to, easy to park and gives some cool photos, so there's no reason not to go, if you're by car, of course.

One of the smallest parks I've ever seen in my life! It has the best views of the city though! We were there when the neighboring street also had cherry blossoms and it was gorgeous. Street parking was busy but not impossible on the middle of a weekday.   No admission fees or bathrooms for that matter.
